texto: What he'd find there, of course, was up to Pete. But he was sure there were magicians in Tampico and leopard-skins and golden thrones in Juba. Dragons and pirates and white temples where magic dwelt. And best of all, the places he didn't know about yet, the ones that would come as surprises. Oh, not entirely pleasant surprises. There should be a hint of peril, a touch of terror, to emphasize the brightness of adventure...("Before I Wake...")
explicacao: Orientação direta de Henry Kuttner sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “What he'd find there, of course, was up to Pete. But he was sure there were magicians in Tampico …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, adventure, fantasy, útil em momentos de fantasy e peril.

frase_original: What he'd find there, of course, was up to Pete. But he was sure there were magicians in Tampico and leopard-skins and golden thrones in Juba. Dragons and pirates and white temples where magic dwelt. And best of all, the places he didn't know about yet, the ones that would come as surprises. Oh, not entirely pleasant surprises. There should be a hint of peril, a touch of terror, to emphasize the brightness of adventure...("Before I Wake...")

texto: A long time ago I lived in Lisbon,' she said, in softly slurred Portuguese that made the name of the city Leesh-boa. 'But before that, meus neto, my tribe was in the mountains where there are only old things, like the trees and the rocks and the streams. There are truths to be learned from the old things -' She hesitated, and her brown, shrunken claw closed over Pete's hand. 'Do you know the truth, Pedrinho?'("Before I Wake...")
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Henry Kuttner: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “A long time ago I lived in Lisbon,' she said, in softly slurred Portuguese that made the name of …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sabedoria, lisbon, magic pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de magic e old.
